ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 102263004 refers to egg white as a substance; no specific hard-boiled preparation code is available in SNOMED CT. Closest parent match used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large hard-boiled chicken egg white weighs approximately 33 g, representing the white portion separated from a single large egg after boiling.

nutrient_source.title: Egg, white, hard-boiled — Singapore HPB Foodo / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172183)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database for hard-boiled egg white. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrient detail cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked egg white (FDC ID 172183). HPB values take precedence for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for hard-boiled egg white
gi_source.source_match_notes: Hard-boiled egg white contains only 0.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI value is assigned per Veritas q4 rule for low/zero-carbohydrate foods. Glycemic load per serving is effectively 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (175 mg/100 g) with potassium offset (112 mg/100 g); zero fat and zero cholesterol
hypertension.guidance: Hard-boiled egg white is suitable for hypertension management at moderate intake; individuals on strict sodium restriction below 1500 mg/day should account for the naturally occurring 175 mg sodium per 100 g and avoid adding salt or high-sodium condiments such as soy sauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0.4 g/100 g), zero sugar, high protein (11.6 g/100 g) supporting glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Hard-boiled egg white has a negligible carbohydrate load and will not raise postprandial blood glucose; its high protein content slows gastric emptying and supports satiety, making it an excellent low-calorie protein choice within a structured meal plan for type 2 diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero cholesterol, zero saturated fat, zero trans fat; lean high-quality protein source
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Hard-boiled egg white contains no cholesterol, no saturated fat, and no trans fat, making it a heart-healthy protein source; individuals with cardiovascular disease can include it freely within a balanced diet, while avoiding high-sodium accompaniments to maintain overall sodium targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No pro-inflammatory fats; however, no positive anti-inflammatory factors (omega-3, polyphenols, antioxidants) present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Hard-boiled egg white is free of pro-inflammatory saturated and trans fats and supports tissue repair through high protein, but it lacks omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, and antioxidants; it should be paired with anti-inflammatory foods such as oily fish or leafy greens for a complete rheumatoid arthritis-friendly meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hard-boiled chicken egg white is the cooked albumen portion of a chicken egg, separated from the yolk after the whole egg has been boiled in its shell in unsalted water until fully set, then deshelled before serving. The white is firm, smooth, and slightly rubbery in texture with a mild, neutral flavour and no discernible fat or cholesterol. It is one of the leanest high-quality protein foods available, providing 11.6 g of complete protein per 100 g at only 49 kcal, with all essential amino acids including leucine and lysine. Carbohydrate content is negligible at 0.4 g per 100 g, making it effectively non-glycaemic. Sodium is naturally present at 175 mg per 100 g, which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension or sodium-restricted diets. Cholesterol is zero, distinguishing egg white from whole egg in cardiovascular dietary planning. Egg white is widely consumed across global cuisines as a standalone protein food, a salad ingredient, a post-workout snack, and a component of calorie-controlled meal plans. It is suitable for halal, kosher, gluten-free, and lacto-vegetarian diets. Clinically, it is recommended for type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease management due to its negligible carbohydrate, zero fat, and zero cholesterol profile. Selenium content (approximately 20 mcg per 100 g) and riboflavin (0.44 mg per 100 g) are notable micronutrients. It is not a source of omega-3 fatty acids or anti-inflammatory polyphenols.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database with 0.95 confidence rating. Secondary micronutrient values cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172183) for cooked egg white. Both sources are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy. The food is a well-characterised, standardised ingredient with consistent values across approved databases.
notes: fat_g is naturally zero: egg white contains no fat by biological composition; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all naturally zero as egg white contains no lipid fraction, confirmed per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 172183. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero: all cholesterol in a chicken egg is contained in the yolk; egg white contains no cholesterol, confirmed zero per HPB and USDA sources. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero: egg white is an animal protein with no plant fibre, confirmed zero per HPB. vitamin_c_mg is naturally zero: egg white contains no ascorbic acid, confirmed per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: vitamin A in eggs is concentrated in the yolk; HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172183) do not report a meaningful RAE value for egg white alone, confirming data unavailability for this specific nutrient in isolated egg white. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: egg white contains negligible vitamin D; all vitamin D in eggs is concentrated in the yolk, confirmed per USDA SR Legacy. vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: egg white contains negligible vitamin E, confirmed per USDA SR Legacy. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.4 g/100 g); gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_index set to null per Veritas q4 rule for low/zero-carbohydrate foods. Sodium at 175 mg/100 g is naturally occurring in egg white and is not from added salt. This record covers the white portion only; nutritional profile differs substantially from whole hard-boiled egg (F01060010) and hard-boiled egg yolk.
